title: प्रदोष व्रत कथा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री प्रदोष व्रत कथा ॥
॥ शिव पुराण अनुसार ॥

॥ प्रदोष व्रत माहात्म्य ॥

सूत जी बोले — हे मुनिगण! अब मैं प्रदोष व्रत का माहात्म्य सुनाता हूँ जो शिव पुराण में भगवान शिव ने स्वयं माता पार्वती को सुनाया था।

त्रयोदशी तिथि को सन्ध्याकाल (प्रदोष काल) में भगवान शिव की पूजा करने से अनन्त फल की प्राप्ति होती है।

॥ कथा प्रारम्भ ॥

प्राचीन काल में विदर्भ देश में एक धर्मात्मा ब्राह्मण अपनी पत्नी के साथ रहता था। उनके कोई सन्तान नहीं थी। उन्होंने अनेक तीर्थ किये, अनेक देवताओं की उपासना की, किन्तु सन्तान सुख प्राप्त न हुआ।

एक दिन नारद मुनि उनके आश्रम में पधारे। ब्राह्मण दम्पति ने उनकी सेवा की और अपना दुःख बताया। नारद मुनि बोले — "हे ब्राह्मण! तुम प्रदोष व्रत करो। प्रत्येक त्रयोदशी को दिन में उपवास करो और सन्ध्याकाल में भगवान शिव की विधिवत पूजा करो।"

"अभिषेक करो — जल, दूध, दही, घी, मधु और शर्करा से। बिल्वपत्र, धतूरा, आक के पुष्प चढ़ाओ। प्रदोष काल में शिव-पार्वती, नन्दी और गणेश सहित शंकर का ध्यान करो। ॐ नमः शिवाय मन्त्र का जाप करो।"

ब्राह्मण दम्पति ने श्रद्धापूर्वक प्रदोष व्रत आरम्भ किया। प्रथम प्रदोष पर उन्होंने शिवलिंग पर अभिषेक किया, बिल्वपत्र चढ़ाये, उपवास रखा और सन्ध्या पूजा की।

छह मास तक निरन्तर प्रदोष व्रत करने के बाद भगवान शिव प्रसन्न हुए। उन्होंने स्वप्न में दर्शन देकर कहा — "हे भक्त! मैं तुम्हारे व्रत और भक्ति से प्रसन्न हूँ। तुम्हें पुत्र रत्न की प्राप्ति होगी जो धर्मात्मा, विद्वान और दीर्घायु होगा।"

यथासमय उन्हें सुन्दर पुत्र की प्राप्ति हुई। वह बालक बड़ा होकर वेदों का ज्ञाता, धर्मात्मा और प्रजापालक राजा बना।

॥ द्वितीय कथा — चन्द्रमा का प्रदोष व्रत ॥

समुद्र मन्थन में चन्द्रमा प्रगट हुए। दक्ष प्रजापति ने अपनी सत्ताईस कन्याएँ (नक्षत्र) चन्द्रमा को ब्याही। किन्तु चन्द्रमा केवल रोहिणी से प्रेम करते थे। अन्य पत्नियों ने पिता से शिकायत की। दक्ष ने चन्द्रमा को श्राप दिया — "तुम क्षयग्रस्त होओगे!"

चन्द्रमा क्षीण होने लगे। व्याकुल होकर उन्होंने नारद मुनि से उपाय पूछा। नारद बोले — "प्रभासक्षेत्र (सोमनाथ) में जाकर भगवान शिव का प्रदोष व्रत करो।"

चन्द्रमा ने प्रभास क्षेत्र में शिवलिंग की स्थापना कर प्रदोष व्रत किया। भगवान शिव प्रसन्न हुए और बोले — "तुम पूर्ण क्षीण नहीं होओगे। कृष्णपक्ष में क्षीण होकर शुक्लपक्ष में पुनः पूर्ण होओगे।" तभी से चन्द्रमा की कलाएँ घटती-बढ़ती हैं।

वही शिवलिंग सोमनाथ महादेव कहलाया — ज्योतिर्लिंगों में प्रथम।

॥ प्रदोष व्रत विधि संक्षेप ॥

त्रयोदशी के दिन प्रातः स्नान करके संकल्प लें।
दिन भर उपवास रखें (फलाहार या निराहार)।
सन्ध्याकाल (प्रदोष काल) में शिवलिंग पर पंचामृत अभिषेक करें।
बिल्वपत्र, धतूरा, आक पुष्प, चन्दन अर्पित करें।
धूप-दीप-नैवेद्य अर्पण करें।
शिव पंचाक्षर मन्त्र 'ॐ नमः शिवाय' का जाप करें।
प्रदोष व्रत कथा सुनें।
ब्राह्मण भोजन कराकर दक्षिणा दें।

॥ फल ॥

सोम प्रदोष (सोमवार) — चन्द्र दोष शान्ति, मन की शान्ति।
भौम प्रदोष (मंगलवार) — रोग निवारण, ऋण मुक्ति।
शनि प्रदोष — सन्तान प्राप्ति, ग्रह शान्ति।
प्रत्येक प्रदोष — शिव कृपा, सर्व मनोकामना पूर्ति, मोक्ष प्राप्ति।

॥ इति श्री प्रदोष व्रत कथा सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Pradosha Vrata Katha ||
|| Shiva Purana Anusara ||

Suta Ji Bole — He Munigana! Ab main Pradosha Vrata ka Mahatmya sunata hun jo Shiva Purana mein Bhagavan Shiva ne svayam Mata Parvati ko sunaya tha.

Trayodashi tithi ko Sandhyakala (Pradosha Kala) mein Bhagavan Shiva ki puja karne se ananta phala ki prapti hoti hai.

|| Katha Prarambha ||

Prachina kala mein Vidarbha desha mein ek dharmatma Brahmana apni patni ke satha rahata tha. Unake koi santana nahin thi. Unhonne aneka tirtha kiye, aneka devataon ki upasana ki, kintu santana sukha prapta na hua.

Eka dina Narada Muni unake ashrama mein padhare. Brahmana dampati ne unaki seva ki aur apna duhkha bataya. Narada Muni bole — "He Brahmana! Tum Pradosha Vrata karo. Pratyeka Trayodashi ko dina mein upavasa karo aur sandhyakala mein Bhagavan Shiva ki vidhivat puja karo."

"Abhisheka karo — jala, dudh, dahi, ghi, madhu aur sharkara se. Bilvapatra, dhatura, aka ke pushpa chadhao. Pradosha kala mein Shiva-Parvati, Nandi aur Ganesha sahita Shankara ka dhyana karo. Om Namah Shivaya mantra ka japa karo."

Brahmana dampati ne shraddhapurvaka Pradosha Vrata arambha kiya. Prathama Pradosha par unhonne Shivalinga par abhisheka kiya, bilvapatra chadhaye, upavasa rakha aur sandhya puja ki.

Chha masa taka nirantara Pradosha Vrata karne ke bada Bhagavan Shiva prasanna hue. Unhonne svapna mein darshana dekar kaha — "He Bhakta! Main tumhare vrata aur bhakti se prasanna hun. Tumhen putra ratna ki prapti hogi jo dharmatma, vidvana aur dirghaayu hoga."

Yathasamaya unhen sundara putra ki prapti hui.

|| Dvitiya Katha — Chandrama Ka Pradosha Vrata ||

Samudra Manthana mein Chandrama pragat hue. Daksha Prajapati ne apni sattaisa kanyayen Chandrama ko byahi. Kintu Chandrama kevala Rohini se prema karate the. Daksha ne Chandrama ko shrapa diya.

Chandrama ne Prabhasa Kshetra mein Shivalinga ki sthapana kara Pradosha Vrata kiya. Bhagavan Shiva prasanna hue aur bole — tum Krishna Paksha mein kshina hokar Shukla Paksha mein punah purna hooge. Wahi Shivalinga Somanatha Mahadeva kahlaya.

|| Iti Shri Pradosha Vrata Katha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: प्रदोष व्रत शिव पुराण से वर्णित है। त्रयोदशी तिथि को सन्ध्याकाल (प्रदोष काल) में भगवान शिव की पूजा से अनन्त फल मिलता है। प्रथम कथा — सन्तानहीन ब्राह्मण ने नारद के कहने पर प्रदोष व्रत किया, शिव ने पुत्र-रत्न दिया। द्वितीय कथा — दक्ष के श्राप से क्षीण चन्द्रमा ने प्रभास क्षेत्र में प्रदोष व्रत किया, शिव ने वरदान दिया कि शुक्लपक्ष में पुनः पूर्ण होओगे — वही सोमनाथ ज्योतिर्लिंग। सोम प्रदोष मन शान्ति, भौम प्रदोष रोग निवारण, शनि प्रदोष सन्तान प्राप्ति — सर्व मनोकामना पूर्ण होती है।
benefits: Sacred vrat katha for Pradosha — observed on every Trayodashi (13th tithi)
Highly pleasing to Lord Shiva when observed during Pradosha Kala (twilight hour)
Invokes progeny to the childless, as narrated in the brahmana couple story
Traditionally recited for removal of planetary afflictions — different days of pradosha have specific benefits
Som Pradosha for mental peace, Bhaum Pradosha for health, Shani Pradosha for children
Connected to the origin of Somnath Jyotirlinga — the first among twelve
description: Pradosh Vrat Katha — प्रदोष व्रत कथा — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Shiva. This sacred composition is sourced from Shiva Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to connected to the origin of Somnath Jyotirlinga — the first among twelve, grant progeny to the childless, as narrated in the Brahmana couple story, and som Pradosha for mental peace, Bhaum Pradosha for health, Shani Pradosha for children. For best results, devotees chant this during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh. Comprising 15 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Pradosh Vrat Katha in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Pradosh Vrat Katha and on which day is the vrat observed?
seoFaqs.answer: Pradosh Vrat Katha is the traditional sacred narrative recited on Pradosha — every Trayodashi (the thirteenth tithi of both the bright and dark fortnights of every lunar month, occurring twice a month). Pradosha-kala specifically refers to the twilight period of approximately 1.5 hours straddling sunset (45 minutes before to 45 minutes after sunset), considered the most sacred time of any day for Shiva worship. The katha is preserved in the Shiva Purana and Skanda Purana. The vrat is a sun-to-moon-rise (or sun-to-night) fast undertaken in honour of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati, with worship at the Pradosha hour and recitation of the Pradosh Vrat Katha being the central observance. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Skanda Purana; Drikpanchang Pradosha.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the traditional Pradosh Vrat Katha narrative?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Pradosh Vrat Katha as preserved in the Shiva Purana tells the story of a poor brahmana widow and her young son who, having lost everything, lived by begging in a town. One evening at twilight she met two royal princes — the sons of the previous king who had been killed and his sons exiled — and gave them shelter. A wise sage instructed all of them to observe the Pradosha Vrat with sincere bhakti. By Shiva's grace, the eldest prince eventually married the daughter of a Gandharva king, regained his ancestral kingdom and enthroned the brahmana boy as his minister. A second classical katha narrates how the gods, terrified by Vritrasura, sought refuge with Shiva at the Pradosha hour and were granted victory through Shiva's tandava — the divine evening dance that the katha invites devotees to witness mentally. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Skanda Purana Pradosha Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Pradosha-kala (twilight) considered so sacred to Shiva?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pradosha-kala — the 1.5-hour twilight period straddling sunset on every Trayodashi — is considered the most sacred time of all in Shiva worship for several traditional reasons. First, the puranic narrative holds that during the Samudra Manthana, Shiva consumed the halahala poison at this very twilight hour to save creation; in gratitude all the gods worship him at twilight on every Trayodashi. Second, Shiva is believed to perform his ananda-tandava (cosmic dance of bliss) on Mount Kailash at this hour with Parvati seated beside him, watched by all the gods and rishis — devotees mentally join this divine assembly. Third, twilight itself is the threshold between day and night, the symbolic union of opposites that Shiva embodies as Ardhanarishvara. Worship at this hour is considered to yield infinitely greater fruit than at any other time. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Skanda Purana; traditional Pradosha mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Som, Bhaum, Shani and other Pradoshas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pradosha Vrat takes its specific name and benefit from the weekday on which the Trayodashi falls. The most significant are: Som Pradosha (Monday Trayodashi) — for mental peace, removal of mind-disturbances and family welfare; Bhaum Pradosha (Tuesday Trayodashi) — for relief from health issues, blood disorders and debts; Saumya Pradosha (Wednesday) — for educational success; Guru Pradosha (Thursday) — for wisdom and progeny; Bhrigu Pradosha (Friday) — for happiness and prosperity; Shani Pradosha (Saturday Trayodashi) — for relief from Saturn's afflictions, sade-sati, dhaiya and for children (considered the most powerful of all); Bhanu Pradosha (Sunday) — for longevity and good health. The Maha Pradosha occurs when Trayodashi falls on a Saturday during Shukla Paksha and is considered especially auspicious. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Drikpanchang Pradosha; traditional Pradosha vrat manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Pradosh Vrat puja performed at home?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional Pradosh Vrat puja begins with a sankalpa at sunrise (morning of Trayodashi) committing to the day-long fast, taking only milk, fruits or a single meal at noon. As Pradosha-kala approaches (about 45 minutes before sunset), the devotee performs a fresh bath, dons clean white or saffron clothes, sits before a Shiva linga or Shiva-Parvati image facing east or north, and performs sandhya-vandana, ganesha-puja, kalasha-sthapana, panchamrita-abhisheka of the linga, then offers bilva-patra (bel leaves), white flowers, dhatura, bhang, sandalwood paste, kumkum, chandan, naivedya (kheer, fruits), and lights a ghee lamp. The Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra is chanted 108 times, followed by Shiva Tandava Stotram, Lingashtakam or Bilvashtakam, and finally the Pradosh Vrat Katha is read aloud to the family. The fast is broken with prasad after sunset. (Sources: Shiva Purana puja vidhi; Pradosha vrat manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does observing the Pradosh Vrat with katha bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries and the closing phalashruti of the Pradosh Vrat Katha promise that sincere observance — including the day-long fast, twilight Shiva puja, and reading of the katha — bestows the highest pleasure of Lord Shiva, grants progeny to the childless (as narrated in the brahmana couple story of the katha), removes planetary afflictions specific to the weekday (Som Pradosha for mental peace, Bhaum Pradosha for health, Shani Pradosha for children and relief from Saturn), connects the devotee to the origin tradition of the Somnath Jyotirlinga (the first of the twelve), grants protection from untimely death (akala-mrityu) when combined with the Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra, removes accumulated paap (sin) of one entire fortnight, and ultimately leads the devotee to Shiva-loka. Observing the vrat for an entire year (24 Pradoshas) followed by an udyapan (completion ritual) is considered to grant moksha. (Sources: Shiva Purana phalashruti; Skanda Purana; traditional Pradosha vrat compendia.)
